137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False

1000	    elif model_type == "llama":   use_fast_convert = True
1001	    elif model_type == "mistral": use_fast_convert = True
1002	    pass
1003	    logger.warning_once(f"Unsloth: Converting {model_type} model. Can use fast conversion = {use_fast_convert}.")
1004	
1005	    # Map quant methods
1006	    new_quantization_method = []
1007	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
1008	        if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = model_dtype
1009	        elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
1010	        elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
1011	        elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
1012	
1013	        # Check if wrong method
1014	        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
1015	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
1016	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
1017	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
1018	            raise RuntimeError(error)
1019	        pass
1020	
1021	        new_quantization_method.append(quant_method)
1022	    pass
1023	    quantization_method = new_quantization_method
1024	
1025	    print_info = \
1026	        f"==((====))==  Unsloth: Conversion from QLoRA to GGUF information\n"\
1027	        f"   {chr(92)}{chr(92)}   /|    [0] Installing llama.cpp might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1028	        f"O^O/ {chr(92)}_/ {chr(92)}    [1] Converting HF to GGUF 16bits might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1029	        f"{chr(92)}        /    [2] Converting GGUF 16bits to {quantization_method} might take 10 minutes each.\n"\
1030	        f' "-____-"     In total, you will have to wait at least 16 minutes.\n'
1031	    print(print_info)
1032	
1033	    # Check first_conversion format
1034	    if   first_conversion == "f16"  : pass
1035	    elif first_conversion == "bf16" : pass
1036	    elif first_conversion == "f32"  : pass
1037	    elif first_conversion == "q8_0" : pass
1038	    else:
1039	        raise RuntimeError(
1040	            f"Unsloth: `first_conversion` can only be one of ['f16', 'bf16', 'f32', 'q8_0'] and not `{first_conversion}`."
1041	        )
1042	    pass
1043	
1044	    # Determine whether the system already has llama.cpp installed and the scripts are executable

1046	    convert_location  = get_executable(["convert-hf-to-gguf.py", "convert_hf_to_gguf.py"])
1047	
1048	    error = 0
1049	    if quantize_location is not None and convert_location is not None:

1052	        print("Unsloth: Installing llama.cpp. This might take 3 minutes...")
1053	        if _run_installer is not None:

1083	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize"):
1084	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/quantize"
1085	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"):
1086	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"

814	def try_execute(commands, force_complete = False):
815	    for command in commands:
816	        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell = True, stdout = subprocess.PIPE, stderr = subprocess.STDOUT, bufsize = 1) as sp:
817	            for line in sp.stdout:
818	                line = line.decode("utf-8", errors = "replace")
819	                if "undefined reference" in line:
820	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
821	                elif "deprecated" in line:
822	                    return "CMAKE"
823	                elif "Unknown argument" in line:
824	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
825	                elif "***" in line:
826	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
827	                print(line, flush = True, end = "")

837	def install_llama_cpp_old(version = -10):
838	    # Download the 10th latest release since the latest might be broken!
839	    # FALLBACK mechanism
840	    releases = subprocess.check_output(["git", "ls-remote", "--tags", "https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp.git"])
841	    releases = releases.decode("utf-8").replace("\t", " ").split("\n")
842	    for i, x in enumerate(releases):
843	        if "refs/tags/b" not in x: break
844	    releases = releases[:i]
845	    latest = releases[-1]
846	    version = releases[version].split(" ")[0]
847	
848	    # Check if the llama.cpp exists
849	    if os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
850	        print(
851	            "**[WARNING]** You have a llama.cpp directory which is broken.\n"\
852	            "Unsloth will DELETE the broken directory and install a new one.\n"\
853	            "Press CTRL + C / cancel this if this is wrong. We shall wait 30 seconds.\n"
854	        )
855	        import time
856	        for i in range(30):
857	            print(f"**[WARNING]** Deleting llama.cpp directory... {30-i} seconds left.")
858	            time.sleep(1)
859	        import shutil
860	        shutil.rmtree("llama.cpp", ignore_errors = True)
861	    pass
862	
863	    # Clone a specific commit
864	    # Also don't use the GPU!
865	    commands = [
866	        "git clone --recursive https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp",
867	        f"cd llama.cpp && git reset --hard {version} && git clean -df",
868	    ]
869	    try_execute(commands)
870	
871	    # Try using MAKE
872	    commands = [
873	        "make clean -C llama.cpp",
874	        f"make all -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} -C llama.cpp",
875	    ]
876	    if try_execute(commands) == "CMAKE":
877	        # Instead use CMAKE
878	        commands = [
879	            "cmake llama.cpp -B llama.cpp/build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF -DGGML_CUDA=OFF -DLLAMA_CURL=ON",
880	            f"cmake --build llama.cpp/build --config Release -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} --clean-first --target {' '.join(LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS)}",
881	            "cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp",
882	            "rm -rf llama.cpp/build",
883	        ]
884	        try_execute(commands)
885	    pass
886	
887	    # Check if successful
888	    if not (
889	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe") or
890	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize") or
891	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize.exe") or
892	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize") or
893	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize") or
894	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize")
895	    ):
896	        raise RuntimeError(
897	            "Unsloth: The file 'llama.cpp/llama-quantize' or `llama.cpp/quantize` does not exist.\n"\
898	            "We've also double checked the building directory under 'llama.cpp/build/bin/'.\n"\
899	            "But we expect this file to exist! Check if the file exists under llama.cpp and investigate the building process of llama.cpp (make/cmake)!"
900	        )
901	    pass
902	pass

937	def get_executable(executables):
938	    # Get system locations (System Path).split(system separator)
939	    system_directories = os.environ.get("PATH").split(os.pathsep)
940	
941	    for directory in system_directories:
942	        for executable in executables:
943	            path = os.path.join(directory, executable)
944	            # Check if the executable exists and is executable
945	            if os.path.exists(path) and os.access(path, os.X_OK): return path
946	        pass
947	    pass
948	    return None

952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False
999	    if not is_sentencepiece:      use_fast_convert = False # Llama-3

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

763	def install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking():
764	    full_command = ["git", "clone", "--recursive", "https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp"]
765	    run_installer = subprocess.Popen(full_command, stdout = subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr = subprocess.STDOUT)
766	    return run_installer

770	def install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking():
771	    # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062
772	    # Weirdly GPU conversion for GGUF breaks??
773	    # env = { **os.environ, "LLAMA_CUDA": "1", }
774	    # Force make clean
775	    check = os.system("make clean -C llama.cpp")
776	    IS_CMAKE = False
777	    if check == 0:
778	        # Uses old MAKE
779	        n_jobs = max(int(psutil.cpu_count()*1.5), 1)
780	        full_command = ["make", "all", "-j"+str(n_jobs), "-C", "llama.cpp"]
781	        IS_CMAKE = False
782	    else:
783	        # Uses new CMAKE
784	        n_jobs = max(int(psutil.cpu_count()), 1) # Use less CPUs since 1.5x faster
785	        check = os.system("cmake llama.cpp -B llama.cpp/build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF -DGGML_CUDA=OFF -DLLAMA_CURL=ON")
786	        if check != 0:
787	            raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp using os.system(...) with error {check}. Please report this ASAP!")
788	        pass
789	        # f"cmake --build llama.cpp/build --config Release -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} --clean-first --target {' '.join(LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS)}",
790	        full_command = [
791	            "cmake", "--build", "llama.cpp/build",
792	            "--config", "Release",
793	            "-j"+str(n_jobs),
794	            "--clean-first",
795	            "--target",
796	        ] + LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS
797	        IS_CMAKE = True
798	    pass
799	    # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed summary report:

## Unsloth LoRA Adapter Save & Export Architecture - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's what I found:

### 1. **LoRA Adapter Saving Methods**

**Main Save Function**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346562929-33q9oz/unsloth/save.py:205`
- `unsloth_save_model()` - Core function supporting three save methods:
  - `"lora"` - Saves only LoRA adapter weights (PEFT format)
  - `"merged_16bit"` - Merges LoRA with base weights and saves as float16
  - `"merged_4bit"` - Merges and keeps 4-bit quantization

**Saving Mechanisms**:
- For LoRA-only: Uses PEFT's `model.save_pretrained()` directly (lines 444-448 in save.py)
- For merged models: Manually builds state_dict by iterating through layers and calling `_merge_lora()` (lines 567-602)
- Push to Hub support via `model.push_to_hub()` wrapper (lines 334-370)

### 2. **LoRA A and B Matrix Access Pattern**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346562929-33q9oz/unsloth/kernels/utils.py:235`
- Function `get_lora_parameters_bias()` returns 6-tuple:
  ```python
  (W, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scaling, bias)
  ```
- LoRA matrices accessed via:
  - `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` - Input projection (shape: `[r, input_dim]`)
  - `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` - Output projection (shape: `[output_dim, r]`)
  - `proj.scaling[adapter]` - Scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)

**Key Implementation** (lines 249-256):
```python
return (
    W,
    getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
    proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
    proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
    proj.scaling[adapter],
    base_layer.bias,
)
```

### 3. **LoRA Merging Process**

**Function**: `_merge_lora()` at line 159 in save.py
- Extracts base weight W and LoRA matrices A, B
- Applies: `W += (A.T @ (s * B.T))` where `s` is the scaling factor
- Uses `W.addmm_()` for efficient fused multiply-add (line 176):
  ```python
  W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
  ```
- Handles quantization states automatically via `fast_dequantize()`

### 4. **Adapter Config Storage**

**Location**: PEFT's `adapter_config.json` (saved alongside model)

**Key Parameters** (from `unsloth/models/llama.py:2598-2610`):
```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,              # LoRA rank
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,    # Scaling factor
    target_modules      = final_modules,  # ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", ...]
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,
    bias                = bias,           # "none" or "all"
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,  # ["lm_head", "embed_tokens"]
)
lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
```

**Config Creation** (line 2617):
```python
lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
```

### 5. **GGUF Export Implementation**

**Primary Function**: `save_to_gguf()` at line 952 in save.py
- **Two-Stage Conversion**:
  1. First converts merged 16-bit HF model to GGUF via `convert_hf_to_gguf.py`
  2. Then quantizes GGUF via `llama-quantize` tool

**Quantization Methods Supported** (from ALLOWED_QUANTS dict, lines 67-107):
- Not quantized: `f32`, `f16`, `bf16`
- Fast: `q8_0` (8-bit)
- Recommended: `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q6_k`
- Other: `q4_0`, `q4_1`, `q5_0`, `q5_1`, `q3_k_*`, etc.

**GGUF Integration**:
- Uses llama.cpp ecosystem for conversion and quantization
- Non-blocking installation of llama.cpp during first save (lines 1835-1873)
- Automatic first-pass conversion selection based on target quantization (lines 1117-1150)

**GGUF Entry Points**:
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - Line 1768
- `save_to_gguf_generic()` - Line 2276

### 6. **FastLanguageModel Class**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346562929-33q9oz/unsloth/models/loader.py:107`
- Inherits from `FastLlamaModel`
- Static method: `from_pretrained()` - Loads base model with LoRA support
- Instance method: `get_peft_model()` - Applies LoRA configuration
- Instance method: `save_pretrained_merged()` - Saves merged weights
- Instance method: `save_pretrained_gguf()` - Exports to GGUF format
- Instance method: `push_to_hub()` - Uploads to Hugging Face Hub

### 7. **Custom LoRA Save Function**

**Location**: `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` at line 2129 in save.py
- Wrapper around `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="lora"`
- Ensures proper directory creation and PEFT format compliance

### 8. **GGML Export (for LoRA adapters)**

**Function**: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` at line 2211 in save.py
- Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format via llama.cpp
- Command: `python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py <lora_dir> <output_file> llama`
- Outputs: `ggml-adapter-model.bin`

### 9. **No ONNX Implementation Found**

**ONNX/opset search results**:
- Only 4 files mention "gguf" or "onnx"
- No ONNX export functionality exists in current codebase
- GGUF is the primary export format

### 10. **Key Export Patterns**

**Flow Diagram**:
```
Model + LoRA Adapters
    ↓
[Save Method Selection]
    ↓
├─ "lora"         → PEFT format (adapter_config.json + lora weights)
├─ "merged_16bit" → Full model in float16 (no quantization_config)
├─ "merged_4bit"  → Full model in 4-bit quantization
└─ "gguf"         → GGUF format via llama.cpp conversion
```

**Important Implementation Notes**:
- LoRA weights are stored as separate files in PEFT format
- Scaling is stored as `lora_alpha / r` in adapter config
- Merged models delete `quantization_config` from config.json to indicate unquantized state (lines 684-691)
- Memory-efficient saving uses disk buffering for large models (lines 588-593)
- All exports support push_to_hub with automatic HF Hub token handling

### File References Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | Core save/export logic |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | LoRA parameter extraction |
| `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | FastLanguageModel class and entry points |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | LoRA config creation (lines 2598-2617) |
| `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` | Save method examples and validation |

This architecture enables flexible model export with support for LoRA-only saving, merged weight export, GGUF quantization, and Hub integration—all while maintaining memory efficiency through intelligent buffer management.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed summary report:

## Unsloth LoRA Adapter Save & Export Architecture - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's what I found:

### 1. **LoRA Adapter Saving Methods**

**Main Save Function**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346562929-33q9oz/unsloth/save.py:205`
- `unsloth_save_model()` - Core function supporting three save methods:
  - `"lora"` - Saves only LoRA adapter weights (PEFT format)
  - `"merged_16bit"` - Merges LoRA with base weights and saves as float16
  - `"merged_4bit"` - Merges and keeps 4-bit quantization

**Saving Mechanisms**:
- For LoRA-only: Uses PEFT's `model.save_pretrained()` directly (lines 444-448 in save.py)
- For merged models: Manually builds state_dict by iterating through layers and calling `_merge_lora()` (lines 567-602)
- Push to Hub support via `model.push_to_hub()` wrapper (lines 334-370)

### 2. **LoRA A and B Matrix Access Pattern**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346562929-33q9oz/unsloth/kernels/utils.py:235`
- Function `get_lora_parameters_bias()` returns 6-tuple:
  ```python
  (W, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scaling, bias)
  ```
- LoRA matrices accessed via:
  - `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` - Input projection (shape: `[r, input_dim]`)
  - `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` - Output projection (shape: `[output_dim, r]`)
  - `proj.scaling[adapter]` - Scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)

**Key Implementation** (lines 249-256):
```python
return (
    W,
    getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
    proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
    proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
    proj.scaling[adapter],
    base_layer.bias,
)
```

### 3. **LoRA Merging Process**

**Function**: `_merge_lora()` at line 159 in save.py
- Extracts base weight W and LoRA matrices A, B
- Applies: `W += (A.T @ (s * B.T))` where `s` is the scaling factor
- Uses `W.addmm_()` for efficient fused multiply-add (line 176):
  ```python
  W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
  ```
- Handles quantization states automatically via `fast_dequantize()`

### 4. **Adapter Config Storage**

**Location**: PEFT's `adapter_config.json` (saved alongside model)

**Key Parameters** (from `unsloth/models/llama.py:2598-2610`):
```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,              # LoRA rank
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,    # Scaling factor
    target_modules      = final_modules,  # ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", ...]
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,
    bias                = bias,           # "none" or "all"
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,  # ["lm_head", "embed_tokens"]
)
lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
```

**Config Creation** (line 2617):
```python
lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
```

### 5. **GGUF Export Implementation**

**Primary Function**: `save_to_gguf()` at line 952 in save.py
- **Two-Stage Conversion**:
  1. First converts merged 16-bit HF model to GGUF via `convert_hf_to_gguf.py`
  2. Then quantizes GGUF via `llama-quantize` tool

**Quantization Methods Supported** (from ALLOWED_QUANTS dict, lines 67-107):
- Not quantized: `f32`, `f16`, `bf16`
- Fast: `q8_0` (8-bit)
- Recommended: `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q6_k`
- Other: `q4_0`, `q4_1`, `q5_0`, `q5_1`, `q3_k_*`, etc.

**GGUF Integration**:
- Uses llama.cpp ecosystem for conversion and quantization
- Non-blocking installation of llama.cpp during first save (lines 1835-1873)
- Automatic first-pass conversion selection based on target quantization (lines 1117-1150)

**GGUF Entry Points**:
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - Line 1768
- `save_to_gguf_generic()` - Line 2276

### 6. **FastLanguageModel Class**

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775346562929-33q9oz/unsloth/models/loader.py:107`
- Inherits from `FastLlamaModel`
- Static method: `from_pretrained()` - Loads base model with LoRA support
- Instance method: `get_peft_model()` - Applies LoRA configuration
- Instance method: `save_pretrained_merged()` - Saves merged weights
- Instance method: `save_pretrained_gguf()` - Exports to GGUF format
- Instance method: `push_to_hub()` - Uploads to Hugging Face Hub

### 7. **Custom LoRA Save Function**

**Location**: `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` at line 2129 in save.py
- Wrapper around `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="lora"`
- Ensures proper directory creation and PEFT format compliance

### 8. **GGML Export (for LoRA adapters)**

**Function**: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` at line 2211 in save.py
- Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format via llama.cpp
- Command: `python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py <lora_dir> <output_file> llama`
- Outputs: `ggml-adapter-model.bin`

### 9. **No ONNX Implementation Found**

**ONNX/opset search results**:
- Only 4 files mention "gguf" or "onnx"
- No ONNX export functionality exists in current codebase
- GGUF is the primary export format

### 10. **Key Export Patterns**

**Flow Diagram**:
```
Model + LoRA Adapters
    ↓
[Save Method Selection]
    ↓
├─ "lora"         → PEFT format (adapter_config.json + lora weights)
├─ "merged_16bit" → Full model in float16 (no quantization_config)
├─ "merged_4bit"  → Full model in 4-bit quantization
└─ "gguf"         → GGUF format via llama.cpp conversion
```

**Important Implementation Notes**:
- LoRA weights are stored as separate files in PEFT format
- Scaling is stored as `lora_alpha / r` in adapter config
- Merged models delete `quantization_config` from config.json to indicate unquantized state (lines 684-691)
- Memory-efficient saving uses disk buffering for large models (lines 588-593)
- All exports support push_to_hub with automatic HF Hub token handling

### File References Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | Core save/export logic |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | LoRA parameter extraction |
| `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | FastLanguageModel class and entry points |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | LoRA config creation (lines 2598-2617) |
| `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` | Save method examples and validation |

This architecture enables flexible model export with support for LoRA-only saving, merged weight export, GGUF quantization, and Hub integration—all while maintaining memory efficiency through intelligent buffer management.

## Unsloth Codebase Structure - Comprehensive Analysis

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main entry point - imports all public APIs
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-installation utilities
├── save.py                      # Model saving/export functionality (2,678 lines)
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template configurations (129KB)
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer utilities and fixes (41KB)
├── trainer.py                  # Training argument configurations
├── import_fixes.py             # Import compatibility fixes
├── models/                      # Core model implementations
│   ├── __init__.py             # Model exports
│   ├── loader.py               # FastLanguageModel & FastVisionModel loaders (45KB)
│   ├── llama.py                # FastLlamaModel base class (3,036 lines)

│   ├── qwen2.py                # FastQwen2Model

│   ├── gemma.py                # FastGemmaModel
│   ├── gemma2.py               # FastGemma2Model
│   ├── granite.py              # FastGraniteModel
│   ├── cohere.py               # FastCohereModel
│   ├── falcon_h1.py            # FastFalconH1Model

│   ├── dpo.py                  # DPO trainer patches
│   ├── rl.py                   # Reinforcement Learning support (45KB)

│   ├── mapper.py               # Model mapping utilities
│   ├── _utils.py               # Model utilities (1,683 lines)
│   └── loader_utils.py         # Loader helper functions
├── kernels/                     # Optimized CUDA kernels
│   ├── fast_lora.py            # LoRA-specific optimizations
│   ├── rope_embedding.py       # RoPE embedding kernels
│   ├── layernorm.py            # LayerNorm optimizations
│   ├── rms_layernorm.py        # RMS LayerNorm optimizations
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py   # Cross-entropy loss kernels
│   ├── flex_attention.py       # Flexible attention patterns
│   ├── geglu.py                # GEGLU activation kernels
│   ├── swiglu.py               # SwiGLU activation kernels
│   └── moe/                    # Mixture of Experts kernels
├── registry/                    # Model registry and configuration
│   ├── registry.py             # Main registry system
│   ├── __init__.py             # Public registry API
│   ├── REGISTRY.md             # Registry documentation
│   ├── loader.py               # Registry loader
│   ├── _llama.py               # Llama model registry
│   ├── _gemma.py               # Gemma model registry
│   ├── _mistral.py             # Mistral model registry
│   ├── _qwen.py                # Qwen model registry
│   ├── _deepseek.py            # Deepseek model registry
│   ├── _phi.py                 # Phi model registry
│   ├── mapper.py               # Model mapper
│   └── _utils.py               # Registry utilities
├── utils/                       # Utility modules
│   ├── __init__.py             
│   └── hf_hub.py               # Hugging Face Hub utilities
└── dataprep/                    # Data preparation modules
    └── [data utilities]
```

### 2. FILES RELATED TO SAVING/EXPORTING MODELS

**Primary file:**
- `/unsloth/save.py` (2,678 lines) - Comprehensive model saving module

**Key functions in save.py:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` - Main save function (line 205)
- `save_to_gguf()` - Save to GGUF format (line 952)
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` - Save merged model (line 1321)
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` - Push merged model to Hub (line 1364)
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - Save as GGUF (line 1768)
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` - Push GGUF to Hub (line 1951)
- `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` - Save LoRA to custom directory (line 2129)
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` - Convert LoRA to GGML (line 2143)
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` - Save LoRA as GGML locally (line 2211)
- `patch_saving_functions()` - Patch model saving methods (line 2569)

### 3. WHERE LORA ADAPTERS ARE SAVED

**LoRA Saving Methods Attached to Models:**

The `patch_saving_functions()` function (line 2569) dynamically patches the model with these custom save methods:

- `model.save_pretrained_merged()` - Save merged LoRA weights
- `model.push_to_hub_merged()` - Push merged weights to Hub  
- `model.save_pretrained_gguf()` - Save as GGUF format
- `model.push_to_hub_gguf()` - Push GGUF to Hub
- `model.save_pretrained_ggml()` - Save LoRA as GGML
- `model.push_to_hub_ggml()` - Push LoRA GGML to Hub
- `model.save_pretrained_torchao()` - Save with TorchAO compression
- `model.push_to_hub()` - Enhanced push_to_hub with Unsloth tags

**Core Implementation Details:**

1. **LoRA-only saving** (line 287): `save_method="lora"` - Uses PEFT's native save_pretrained
2. **Merged 16-bit saving** (line 288): `save_method="merged_16bit"` - Merges LoRA into float16 weights
3. **Merged 4-bit saving** (line 289): `save_method="merged_4bit"` - Merges LoRA into int4 weights
4. **GGUF conversion** (line 952): Uses llama.cpp for quantization with multiple quantization methods

### 4. EXISTING EXPORT METHODS

The codebase supports multiple export formats:

**Merged Formats:**
- `merged_16bit` - Float16 merged weights (ideal for GGUF/llama.cpp)
- `merged_4bit` - Int4 merged weights (ideal for DPO/HF inference)
- `lora` - LoRA adapters only (ideal for HF inference, minimal size)

**GGUF/Quantized Formats:**
- Quantization methods: `fast_quantized`, `quantized`, `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q8_0`, `f16`, `bf16`, etc.
- Supports all llama.cpp quantization schemes (17+ quantization types)
- Uses llama.cpp for actual conversion

**GGML Format:**
- Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format for use with llama.cpp
- Uses `llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py`

**TorchAO Format:**
- Custom compression using TorchAO library
- Function: `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 2502)

### 5. MAIN MODEL CLASSES

**Base Model Classes:**

1. **FastLlamaModel** (`/unsloth/models/llama.py`, line 1778)
   - Base class for most models
   - Key methods:
     - `pre_patch()` - Pre-initialization patching
     - `from_pretrained()` (line 1816) - Load model with LoRA
   - Patches transformer attention, RoPE, layer norms for optimization

2. **FastLanguageModel** (`/unsloth/models/loader.py`, line 107)
   - High-level API for loading any supported model
   - Static method: `from_pretrained()` - Auto-detects model type
   - Supports: Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma, Granite, Cohere, Falcon, etc.

3. **FastVisionModel** (`/unsloth/models/loader.py`, line 995)
   - Vision-language model support
   - Methods: `from_pretrained()` for vision models

4. **FastModel** (`/unsloth/models/loader.py`, line 526)
   - Advanced model loader for 8-bit/16-bit training
   - Fallback for models not supporting 4-bit training

**Architecture-specific Model Classes:**
- `FastMistralModel` - Mistral architecture
- `FastQwen2Model` - Qwen2 architecture  
- `FastQwen3Model` - Qwen3 architecture
- `FastQwen3MoeModel` - Qwen3 Mixture of Experts
- `FastGemmaModel` - Gemma architecture
- `FastGemma2Model` - Gemma2 architecture
- `FastGraniteModel` - Granite architecture
- `FastCohereModel` - Cohere architecture
- `FastFalconH1Model` - Falcon H1 architecture

### 6. PUBLIC API EXPORTS (`__init__.py`)

**Main exports from `/unsloth/__init__.py`:**

```python
from .models import *                    # All model classes
from .save import *                      # Saving functions:
    # - unsloth_save_model
    # - save_to_gguf
    # - patch_saving_functions
    # - create_huggingface_repo
    # - print_quantization_methods
from .chat_templates import *            # Chat template utilities
from .tokenizer_utils import *           # Tokenizer fixes
from .trainer import *                   # Training utilities
```

**Key imports from `/unsloth/models/__init__.py`:**
```python
FastLlamaModel
FastLanguageModel           # Main user-facing API
FastVisionModel
FastTextModel
FastModel
FastMistralModel
FastQwen2Model
FastQwen3Model
FastQwen3MoeModel
FastGemmaModel
FastGemma2Model
FastGraniteModel
FastFalconH1Model
PatchDPOTrainer
PatchKTOTrainer
PatchFastRL
__version__
```

### KEY FILE LOCATIONS FOR YOUR STUDY

**Critical files to understand:**

1. `/unsloth/save.py` - Complete saving/export pipeline
2. `/unsloth/models/loader.py` - Model loading and LoRA initialization
3. `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - Base model patching and LoRA application
4. `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` - Model utilities and PEFT integration
5. `/unsloth/trainer.py` - Training integration
6. `/unsloth/models/vision.py` - Vision model handling

**For LoRA-specific code:**
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Optimized LoRA kernels
- `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` - PEFT model preparation (line 823: `prepare_model_for_kbit_training()`)

**For model registry:**
- `/unsloth/registry/registry.py` - Model registry system
- `/unsloth/registry/_llama.py` - Llama registry entries
- `/unsloth/registry/loader.py` - Registry-based model loading

This structure allows Unsloth to provide optimized LoRA training and flexible export options while maintaining compatibility with the broader Hugging Face ecosystem.

   - High-level API for loading any supported model
   - Static method: `from_pretrained()` - Auto-detects model type
   - Supports: Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma, Granite, Cohere, Falcon, etc.

3. **FastVisionModel** (`/unsloth/models/loader.py`, line 995)
   - Vision-language model support
   - Methods: `from_pretrained()` for vision models

4. **FastModel** (`/unsloth/models/loader.py`, line 526)
   - Advanced model loader for 8-bit/16-bit training
   - Fallback for models not supporting 4-bit training

**Architecture-specific Model Classes:**
- `FastMistralModel` - Mistral architecture
- `FastQwen2Model` - Qwen2 architecture  
- `FastQwen3Model` - Qwen3 architecture
- `FastQwen3MoeModel` - Qwen3 Mixture of Experts
- `FastGemmaModel` - Gemma architecture
- `FastGemma2Model` - Gemma2 architecture
- `FastGraniteModel` - Granite architecture
- `FastCohereModel` - Cohere architecture
- `FastFalconH1Model` - Falcon H1 architecture

### 6. PUBLIC API EXPORTS (`__init__.py`)

**Main exports from `/unsloth/__init__.py`:**

```python
from .models import *                    # All model classes
from .save import *                      # Saving functions:
    # - unsloth_save_model
    # - save_to_gguf
    # - patch_saving_functions
    # - create_huggingface_repo
    # - print_quantization_methods
from .chat_templates import *            # Chat template utilities
from .tokenizer_utils import *           # Tokenizer fixes
from .trainer import *                   # Training utilities
```

**Key imports from `/unsloth/models/__init__.py`:**
```python
FastLlamaModel
FastLanguageModel           # Main user-facing API
FastVisionModel
FastTextModel
FastModel
FastMistralModel
FastQwen2Model
FastQwen3Model
FastQwen3MoeModel
FastGemmaModel
FastGemma2Model
FastGraniteModel
FastFalconH1Model
PatchDPOTrainer
PatchKTOTrainer
PatchFastRL
__version__
```

### KEY FILE LOCATIONS FOR YOUR STUDY

**Critical files to understand:**

1. `/unsloth/save.py` - Complete saving/export pipeline
2. `/unsloth/models/loader.py` - Model loading and LoRA initialization
3. `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - Base model patching and LoRA application
4. `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` - Model utilities and PEFT integration
5. `/unsloth/trainer.py` - Training integration
6. `/unsloth/models/vision.py` - Vision model handling

**For LoRA-specific code:**
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Optimized LoRA kernels
- `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` - PEFT model preparation (line 823: `prepare_model_for_kbit_training()`)

**For model registry:**
- `/unsloth/registry/registry.py` - Model registry system
- `/unsloth/registry/_llama.py` - Llama registry entries
- `/unsloth/registry/loader.py` - Registry-based model loading

This structure allows Unsloth to provide optimized LoRA training and flexible export options while maintaining compatibility with the broader Hugging Face ecosystem.

from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import sys
import requests
import torch
import os
import shutil
import pickle
import gc
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias

        from huggingface_hub.utils import get_token

        from huggingface_hub.utils._token import get_token

pass

__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s
LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli",]

# Check environments
keynames = "\n" + "\n".join(os.environ.keys())
IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT  = "\nCOLAB_"  in keynames
IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT = "\nKAGGLE_" in keynames
KAGGLE_TMP = "/tmp"
del keynames

# Weights
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19

{

    "f16"     : "Float16  - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
    "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
    "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
    "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
    "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
    "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
    "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
    "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
    "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
    # "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization", # Not supported sadly
    # "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
    # "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
    "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
}

def print_quantization_methods():
    for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
        print(f'"{key}"  ==> {value}')
    pass
pass


def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False
    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
    created_folder = False
    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
        created_folder = True
        os.makedirs(file_location)
    pass
    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
        sentencepiece_model = True
    pass
    if created_folder:
        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
    return sentencepiece_model
pass


def _free_cached_model(model):
    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)

    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
    pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

    # Push to hub

# Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()

    if commit_description is None:
        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"
    pass

    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
    pass

    # First check for a token!
    if push_to_hub:
        from huggingface_hub import whoami
        try:
            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
        except:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
            )
        pass
    pass

    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)

    # Clean memory up first
    for _ in range(3):
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
        gc.collect()
    pass

    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
        )
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":

        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
        print("This might take 5 minutes...")

        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
            model = model.merge_and_unload()
        pass
        print("Done.")
    pass

    if tags is not None:
        assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
        tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
    else:
        tags = ["unsloth",]
    pass
    save_pretrained_settings["tags"] = tags

    if ((save_method == "lora") or (save_method == "merged_4bit")) and push_to_hub:
        if token is None:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Unsloth: Pushing to HF requires a token. Pass `token = 'hf_....'`\n"\
                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens."
            )
        pass

        if save_method == "lora":
            print("Unsloth: Saving LoRA adapters. Please wait...")
        elif save_method == "merged_4bit":
            print("Unsloth: Saving 4bit Bitsandbytes model. Please wait...")
        pass

        # Update model tag
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            model, save_directory, token,
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = private,
        )

        getattr(model, "original_push_to_hub", model.push_to_hub)\
        (
            repo_id            = save_directory,
            use_temp_dir       = use_temp_dir,
            commit_message     = commit_message,
            private            = private,

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)

            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)

            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)

        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model

def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")
pass

if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
    return X
pass

        if quant_state is None: return W

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()

def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    pass

    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = True
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["repo_id"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

        if save_method != "lora": print(" This might take 10 minutes for Llama-7b...", end = "")

        # [TODO] Is this correct?

        pass

    pass

    # If push_to_hub, we must remove the .../ part of a repo
    username = None
    if push_to_hub and "/" in save_directory:

        # +1 solves absolute path issues
        new_save_directory = save_directory
        username = new_save_directory[:new_save_directory.find("/")]
        new_save_directory = new_save_directory[new_save_directory.find("/")+1:]
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            new_save_directory = os.path.join(KAGGLE_TMP, new_save_directory[new_save_directory.find("/")+1:])
            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: You are pushing to hub in Kaggle environment.\n"\
                f"To save memory, we shall move {save_directory} to {new_save_directory}"
            )
        else:
            logger.warning_once(
                f"Unsloth: You are pushing to hub, but you passed your HF username = {username}.\n"\
                f"We shall truncate {save_directory} to {new_save_directory}"
            )

        save_pretrained_settings["save_directory"] = new_save_directory
        tokenizer_save_settings ["save_directory"] = new_save_directory
        save_directory = new_save_directory
    pass

    print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 16bit...")

    # Determine max RAM usage minus sharding
    max_ram = psutil.virtual_memory().available
    sharded_ram_usage = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024
    if type(max_shard_size) is str:
        gb_found = re.match(r"([0-9]{1,})[\s]{0,}GB", max_shard_size, flags = re.IGNORECASE)
        mb_found = re.match(r"([0-9]{1,})[\s]{0,}MB", max_shard_size, flags = re.IGNORECASE)
        if   gb_found: sharded_ram_usage = int(gb_found.group(1)) * 1024 * 1024 * 1024
        elif mb_found: sharded_ram_usage = int(mb_found.group(1)) * 1024 * 1024
    elif type(max_shard_size) is int:
        sharded_ram_usage = sharded_ram_usage
    pass

    # Switch to our fast saving modules if it's a slow PC!
    n_cpus = psutil.cpu_count(logical = False)
    if n_cpus is None: n_cpus = psutil.cpu_count()
    if n_cpus is None: n_cpus = 1

    if safe_serialization is None:
        safe_serialization = True
        save_pretrained_settings["safe_serialization"] = safe_serialization

    elif safe_serialization and (n_cpus <= 2):
        logger.warning_once(
            f"Unsloth: You have {n_cpus} CPUs. Using `safe_serialization` is 10x slower.\n"\
            f"We shall switch to Pytorch saving, which might take 3 minutes and not 30 minutes.\n"\
            f"To force `safe_serialization`, set it to `None` instead.",
        )
        safe_serialization = False
        save_function = fast_save_pickle
        save_pretrained_settings["safe_serialization"] = safe_serialization
        save_pretrained_settings["save_function"]      = save_function
    pass

    # Only safe_serialization uses more RAM
    if safe_serialization:
        max_ram -= sharded_ram_usage
    else:
        max_ram -= sharded_ram_usage*0.25 # Uses much less
    pass

    max_ram = int(max(0, max_ram) * maximum_memory_usage)
    print(f"Unsloth: Will use up to "\
          f"{round(max_ram/1024/1024/1024, 2)} out of "\
          f"{round(psutil.virtual_memory().total/1024/1024/1024, 2)} RAM for saving.")

    # Move temporary_location to /tmp in Kaggle
    if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
        temporary_location = os.path.join(KAGGLE_TMP, temporary_location)

    # Max directory for disk saving
    if not os.path.exists(temporary_location):
        os.makedirs(temporary_location)
    pass

    # Check if Kaggle or Colab, since only 20GB of Disk space allowed.
    if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT or IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT:
        # We free up 4GB of space
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: Kaggle/Colab has limited disk space. We need to delete the downloaded\n"\
            "model which will save 4-16GB of disk space, allowing you to save on Kaggle/Colab."
        )
        _free_cached_model(internal_model)
    pass

    # HF also uses a OrderedDict
    from collections import OrderedDict
    state_dict = OrderedDict()

    torch_dtype = dtype_from_config(internal_model.config)
    if type(torch_dtype) is str:
        if   torch_dtype ==  "float16": torch_dtype = torch.float16
        elif torch_dtype == "bfloat16": torch_dtype = torch.bfloat16
    pass

    # Check modules to save float32 dtype
    state_dict["model.embed_tokens.weight"] = internal_model.model.embed_tokens.weight.data.to(torch_dtype)

    max_vram = int(torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0).total_memory * maximum_memory_usage)

    print("Unsloth: Saving model... This might take 5 minutes ...")

    from tqdm import tqdm as ProgressBar
    for j, layer in enumerate(ProgressBar(internal_model.model.layers)):
        for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:
            proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
            name = f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"
            W, bias = _merge_lora(proj, name)

            # Bias term
            if bias is not None:
                state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.bias"] = bias
            pass

            if (torch.cuda.memory_allocated() + W.nbytes) < max_vram:
                # Save to GPU memory
                state_dict[name] = W
            # [TODO] Saving to RAM seems to leak memory???
            # elif (max_ram - W.nbytes) > 0:
            #     # Save to CPU memory
            #     logger.warning_once(f"We will save to RAM and not VRAM now.")
            #     state_dict[name] = W.to("cpu", non_blocking = True, copy = True)
            #     max_ram = max(max_ram - W.nbytes, 0)
            else:
                # Save to Disk
                logger.warning_once("\nWe will save to Disk and not RAM now.")
                filename = os.path.join(temporary_location, f"{name}.pt")
                torch.save(W, filename, pickle_module = pickle, pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,)
                # weights_only = True weirdly fails?
                state_dict[name] = torch.load(filename, map_location = "cpu", mmap = True, weights_only = False)
        pass
        for item in LLAMA_LAYERNORMS:
            try:
                # Skip for Gemma 2
                state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"] = eval(f"layer.{item}.weight.data")
            except:
                continue
        pass
    pass

    state_dict["model.norm.weight"] = internal_model.model.norm.weight.data
    # Check for modules_to_save float32 dtype

    # Check for tied weights
    if internal_model.model.embed_tokens.weight.data_ptr() != internal_model.lm_head.weight.data_ptr():
        state_dict["lm_head.weight"] = internal_model.lm_head.weight.data.to(torch_dtype)
    pass

    # All tensors MUST be type torch.Tensor and not torch.nn.parameter.Parameter
    for key, value in state_dict.items():
        if hasattr(value, "data"): state_dict[key] = value = value.data
        if type(value) is not torch.Tensor:
            logger.warning_once(f"Unsloth: {key} is not a Tensor but a {type(value)}.")
        pass
    pass

    # Edit save_pretrained_settings
    # [TODO] _create_repo has errors due to **kwargs getting accepted
    save_pretrained_settings["state_dict"] = state_dict

    # commit_description does not seem to work?
    what_to_delete = ("use_temp_dir", "commit_message", "create_pr", "revision", "commit_description", "tags",) \
        if not push_to_hub else \
        ("use_temp_dir", "create_pr", "revision", "tags", "commit_description",)
    for deletion in what_to_delete:
        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
    pass
    if hasattr(model, "add_model_tags"):
        model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    # Update model tag
    if push_to_hub:
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            model, save_pretrained_settings["save_directory"], token,
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = username, private = private,
